title: limonium × virgatoformis
intro: Limonium × virgatoformis, also known as sea lavender or sweet-scented sea lavender, is a hybrid plant species within the Plumbaginaceae family. It is primarily grown for its ornamental value and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Limonium × virgatoformis include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. The herbal preparations of Limonium × virgatoformis include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to create topical treatments and inhalants for respiratory issues and skin irritations.
